| 3.00 |  5-1. Initial Search Service.  5－1－2 【Service Overview】.  Wording correction of the recommended route. Changed "Narrow road avoidance" to "Easy to pass narrow roads" setting.  Changed the price calculation from Taiwan to the whole for general land use.  【Service Requirements】. o Added search conditions for the nearest destination section. o *21A reflection of Upgrade (Ver.1.8). o Added to the effect that the default route type is replaced by user operation. o List of default 3 routes added. It is stated that you can edit the destination point on the full route screen. o *21A reflection of Upgrade (Ver.1.8). o Added a list of use cases with the same arrival points.  5－1－5 [Exception operation (abnormal system)]. o Added a list of display examples when offline and outside the cache area failed to search due to factors.  5-2 Reroute Service. 5－4. Learning Route Search Service.  5－4－2 【Service Overview】. o Added the description that route learning will be carried out locally and data of the learning results will be uploaded to the center. o Added a description on how to manage route training data on the center side. o Added the description that the data of the route learning result will be discarded according to user request. o Added Service Requirements to 5-4 Learning Route Search Service Section.  5-5. Stopover setting (number of settings) service.  5－5－2 【Service Requirements】. Removed wording related to additional position designation when adding a stopover.  5－5－3 [Requirements for HMI specifications]. o Added wording about adding a stopover in the stop editing screen. o Added wording related to position change on the stop editing screen. o Added a description about intuition route operation.  5－7． SALIK Information Service.  5－7－2 【Service Overview】. o Delete the price display (go to 5-1).  5－8． E-toll guidance service.  5－8－2 【Service Overview】. o Delete the price display (go to 5-1).  Added Service Requirements to 5-9 Rodizio Notification Display (Environmental Zone) Service Section.  5－10． ERP Guidance Service.  5－10－2 【Service Overview】. o Delete the price display (go to 5-1). 5－11. Technical terms and coined words used in this book are listed below.
本書で使用する専門用語、造語について以下に列挙する。
[START_TABLE_CONTENT] | Term　用語 | Explanation　説明 |
| --- | --- |
| Initial exploration. Initial search. 初期探索 | Initial exploration of the first search after setting up your destination (including transit points). 目的地(通過点を含む)設定後、最初に行う探索を初期探索 |

heading_text: 1． Aim of service　サービスの狙い
content:
Provide services to assist in the movement (from the place of departure to the destination), which is the essence of the service of a moving car.
The way to the destination varies depending on the user, usage scene, or situation.
Therefore, provide routes that suit the user's preferences, such as quickly, cheaply, safely, and efficiently.
移動体である車のサービスの本質である移動(出発地から目的地まで)の支援を行うための
サービスを提供する。
目的地までの行き方はユーザ個々、あるいは利用シーンによって様々であり、状況に応じて
早く、安く、安心に、効率的になど、ユーザの好みに合った経路を提供する。

heading_text: 2． Main usage scenes　主な利用シーン
content:
(1) When the user sets the destination and goes out.
(2) When you want to choose from routes with each characteristic such as fast, cheap, safe, and efficient.
(3) When the user does not travel according to the guide route to the destination.
(4) When a route different from the user's preference and circumstances is searched, such as a high toll for a route traveling on a toll road.
(5) When you want to avoid dangerous roads that require driving skills due to complex road configurations.
(6) There are many things to do in one outing, so when you want to set your destination at once.
(7) When you want to guide the route that is not caught in the traffic jam.
(8) When you want to draw a route that avoids seasonal regulations.
(9) When you want to draw a route that has no borders.
(10) When you want the route to be drawn to the road that the user always passes.
▲1.10
①ユーザが目的地を設定して出かける時。
②早く、安く、安心に、効率的といったそれぞれの特徴を持つルートの中から選びたい時。
③ユーザが目的地への案内ルート通りに走行しなかった時。
④有料道路を走行するルートの通行料金が高いなど、ユーザの好み・事情と異なるルートが探索された時。
⑤道路構成が複雑で運転技術を要する危険な道路は避けて欲しい時。
⑥１度の外出でいくつもの用事があるので、１度に目的地を設定したい時。
⑦渋滞に巻き込まれないルートを案内して欲しい時。
⑧季節規制を避けるルートを引きたい時。
⑨国境をまたがないルートを引きたい時。
⑩ユーザがいつも通る道路にルートを引いて欲しい時。

(1) Initial search service
The way to the destination varies depending on the user or the usage scene, and depending on the situation
Provide multiple routes as a general search result, such as fast, cheap, secure, and efficient.
（１）初期探索サービス
目的地までの行き方はユーザ個々、あるいは利用シーンによって様々であり、状況に応じて
早く、安く、安心に、効率的になど、一般的な探索結果として複数の経路を提供する。
<NA/EU/AU>
The way to the destination varies depending on the user, usage scene, or situation.
Provide 3 optimized routes based on the customer’s current user settings and previous driving history.
SDK shall contain an API that allows for route server to define route preferences/suggestions on a per user basis.
(2) Reroute service
When you miss a route in the middle of a guide, such as a difficult intersection, or when the route is intentionally missed,
Provide an alternate route immediately so that you know which route to go.
（２）リルートサービス
難交差点など案内中経路を間違えて外れてしまったときや、経路を意図的に外れたときに、
進むべき経路が分からなくなる為、代替の経路を即座に提供する。
(3) Search service considering detour areas
Roads that do not suit the user's preferences, roads that are complex in the structure of roads and require driving skills, etc.
Provide a means to avoid.
（３）迂回エリア考慮探索サービス
ユーザの嗜好に合わない道路や道路構成が複雑で運転技術を要する道路などを
回避するための手段を提供する。
(4) Learning route search service
Route learning is carried out locally, and by regularly uploading the data of route learning results to the center.
（４）学習ルート探索サービス
ルート学習結果が反映できる場合、ルート学習経路と入れ替えて提供する。
(5) Stopover setting (Number of settings) Service
Provide a way to set multiple destinations at the same time when there are many errands on a single outing.
<JP/ROW>
In addition, if there is a road you want to pass, it will also provide a means that can be specified on a road basis.
（５）経由地設定(設定数)サービス
1度の外出でいくつもの用事があるときに、同時に複数の目的地を設定できる手段を提供する。
また、通りたい道路がある場合は道路単位での指定ができる手段も提供する。
(6) Traffic jam consideration search service
To help you reach your destination faster and avoid the hassle of navigating traffic areas,
and provide a path that avoids traffic jams that are expected to occur on the route.
（６）渋滞考慮探索サービス
より早く目的地に到着できるよう、また、渋滞区間を通る煩わしさを回避できるよう、現在の渋滞
および経路上に発生することが予測される渋滞を避けた経路を提供する。
(7) SALIK Guidance Service
In Dubai, there is a system called "SALIK" that charges when you pass through the gate.
The SALIK gate may not be noticed, so it provides a service that notifies the user.
(７) SALIK案内サービス
ドバイ市内には、ゲートを通過すると料金が課金されるシステム"SALIK"がある。
SALIKゲートは気付かない可能性もあるため、ユーザに通知するサービスを提供する。
(8) e-toll guidance service
South African highways have an "E-toll" system that charges when they pass through gates.
The E-toll gating may not be aware, so it provides a service that notifies the user.
(８) e-toll案内サービス
南アフリカの高速道路には、ゲートを通過すると料金が課金されるシステム"e-toll"がある。
e-tollゲートは気づかない可能性もあるため、ユーザに通知するサービスを提供する。
(9) Rodizio Notification Display (EnvironmentalZone Message)
When using a road in the Environmental Zone that is subject to entry restriction (Rodizio) on a route, it is possible to make a decision for the user to change the route in advance as necessary by informing that fact at the full route screen.
(９) Rodizioお知らせ表示（EnvironmentalZoneメッセージ）
ルート上で進入規制（Rodizio）対象であるEnvironmental Zone内の道路を使用している場合、全ルート画面時にその旨を知らせることにより、必要に応じてユーザが事前にルートを変更するための判断ができる。
(10) ERP Warning Service
If the route is using a road in the ERP warning area, inform the user before the start of the invitation.
(１０)ERP警告サービス
ルートがERP警告区域内の道路を使用している場合、案内開始前にユーザにその旨を知らせる。
▲1.10
(12)   HOV/HOT Route Service
▲1.10
Routes are automatically calculated to allow or avoid HOV/HOT lanes based on the # of passengers in the vehicle. ▲1.10
(12)  HOV/HOT ルートサービス
自動車の搭乗者番号をもとに、HOV/HOTレーンの利用可否を自動でルート計算する。
(13)  Co2 Minimum Route Service
Using gradient information and traffic jam information possessed by map data, co2 minimum route considering power consumption per link is provided.
(1３) Co2ミニマムルートサービス
地図データが持つ勾配情報、渋滞情報を用いてリンク単位で消費電量を考慮したCo2ミニマムルートを提供する。
<NA/EU/AU>
(14)  Predictive/Preferred Route Learning Service
A route learning service that records both route shape preferences and route interval scheduling in order to predict where a user will drive and provide them with routes tailored to their personal preferences. ▲1.10
(14)  ルート予測/学習サービス
ルート形式とルートスケジューリングを記録し、個人の好みに合わせたルートを提供する。

・ Real time alternate routes shall be displayed on map screen
・ User can select alternate route by clicking on it/tapping on word balloon
・ Selected alternate route becomes the active route. Previously active and alternate routes shall remain as alternate routes for the user to select.
・ User can steer vehicle towards the desired alternate route to change to that route.
・ Route that user steered the vehicle shall be the active route.
・ System shall calculate and show alternate routes to reach a destination and User can see 1 candidate route.
・ ETA difference from the candidate route is displayed by word balloon.
・ User can see up to 3 candidate routes and see how long time of ETA is different from the highlighted route via word balloon in the map.
・ Candidate route is the fastest route unless a preferred route exists for this source and destination at the current time of day in which case the preferred route would become first suggested route.
・ Route 2 and 3 are the optimal routes through different areas or paths. These routes might be 1 – 7 minutes longer as compared to the first route and might add or reduce couple of miles more to reach the destination
o Route 2 is shortest route (distance wise)
o Route 3 is balanced route as compared to route 1 and 2.
・ If a route contains a waypoint prior to the final destination, alternate routes shall be shown leading to that waypoint, not the final destination. Once the waypoint is reached, alternate routes to the final destination will be calculated and shown (or to next waypoint if multiple waypoints are added as an option in the future).
・ “Faster Route” banner shall be displayed only when the alternative route is 5 mins or faster than the current route
・ Alternate routes shall be calculated using the same route logics as those used to calculate 3 routes when the user sets a destination. Real time alternate routes shall use the logics that are not used by the active route. For example, if the active route is calculated based on the fastest route logic, two real time alternate routes shall be calculated based on short and balanced route logic.
・ If the calculated real time alternate route is duplicate of the active route or exiting alternate route, the route shall not be displayed to the user.
・ Existing real time alternate route shall be discarded, and new real time alternate route is created when:
o The CVP moved past the point where the alternate route branch off from the active route
o The user steers vehicle towards the alternate route and the alternate route becomes the active route
o The active route has changed due to:
 The user deviated from the main route
 The user changed the navi settings
 The user added/replaced the waypoint, changed order of destination/waypoint, or removed destination/waypoint
 The navi switched to the new route because of road closure
 The user accepted the new route proposed via “Faster route” banner or “Congestion ahead” banner

children.heading_text: Service requirements　サービス要件　▲1.20
children.content:
・ The search function is to memorize the running road from the departure point to the final destination.
・ The search function is a breakaway link when the user runs away from the initial path,
Remember the return link when you ride the initial route and the road between them separately.
・ Record the information in between the user when returning to the original route after leaving the route.
・ The search function has a link column of the initial route search result that releases / returns the route learning information.
If you are passing at the same time, swap the route of the interval with the route learning path.
・ The learning result is reflected by running the same route three times. In addition, it is not necessary to be three times in a row.
・ If there are multiple learning routes for the same departure point or destination, consider the thing with a new update date and time
・ When updating the map, determine the link shape and update the learning result.
・ Return point to withdrawal that corresponds to the recommended initial route at the time ⇒ route search
If found, replace it with the learned path that holds the interval.
Inconsistencies in search types occur when reflecting learning routes possible, but acceptable.
・ When reflecting the learning route, consider the route preference (search conditions for user selection) and reflect it.
(In the case of a border refusal setting, route learning passing through the border is not reflected.)
・ On the center route, learning according to the time of day is also possible.
・ Keep the maximum number of training data for 100 routes.
・ Do not learn the route to stop at the service area and parking area.
・探索機能は出発地から最終目的地までの走行道路を記憶すること。
・探索機能は初期経路からユーザが離脱走行した時の離脱リンクと、
初期経路へ乗線した時の復帰リンクとその間の走行道路をセットで別に記憶すること。
・ユーザがルートから離脱後、元のルートに復帰時に速やかにその間の情報を記録すること。
・探索機能は初期経路探索結果のリンク列がルート学習情報の離脱リンク／復帰リンクを
同時に通過している場合、その区間の経路をルート学習経路と入れ替えること。
・3回同一ルートを走行したことで学習結果が反映される。尚、3回連続でなくても良い。
・同じ出発地・目的地に対して複数の学習ルートがある場合は更新日時が新しい物を考慮する。
・地図更新時はリンク形状を判断して学習結果を更新すること。
・ルート探索時の推奨初期ルートに該当する離脱⇒復帰点が
見つかった場合は、その区間を保持している学習した経路に差し替えを行う。
学習ルートを反映した場合、探索種別の矛盾が発生する可能性あるが、許容する。
・学習ルートを反映する場合、ルートプリファレンス(ユーザ選択の探索条件)を考慮して反映すること。
(国境通過拒否設定の場合、国境を通過するルート学習は反映しないなど。)
・センタールートにおいては時間帯に応じた学習も可能にする。
・学習データの最大件数は100ルート分保持すること。
・サービスエリアおよびパーキングエリアに立ち寄るルートを学習しないこと。
A specific learning case is shown below.
具体的学習ケースを以下に示す。
【Cases where learning including SAPA is not studied]
【SAPAを含む学習しないケース】
User driving: High-speed main line (withdrawal) →SAPA → high-speed main line (return)
Route learning result: High-speed main line
ユーザ走行：高速本線(離脱)→SAPA→高速本線(復帰)
ルート学習結果：高速本線
User driving: High-speed main line (withdrawal) → JCT→ high-speed main line→ SAPA→ high-speed main line → JCT → high-speed main line (return)
Route learning result: High-speed main line → JCT→ high-speed main line → JCT → high-speed main line
ユーザ走行：高速本線(離脱)→JCT→高速本線→SAPA→高速本線→JCT→高速本線(復帰)
ルート学習結果：高速本線→JCT→高速本線→JCT→高速本線

children.heading_text: Service requirements　サービス要件
children.content:
・ If the user adds a destination, the route type should be the last route type before addition.
・ユーザが目的地追加した場合、ルート種類は、追加前のラストのルート種類とすること。
・ The ability to add, remove, and rearrange stops before starting the guide.
・案内開始前に経由地を追加、削除、並び替えができること。
・ If two or more destinations are set, the route to each destination will be explored with the same route type. It is not possible to change the route type or search conditions between each destination.
・目的地が２つ以上設定された場合、各目的地までのルートは同一のルート種類で探索する。各目的地間のルート種類変更、探索条件変更は出来ないものとする。
・ If the search condition is changed by user request, the search function should be searched by the specified route type.
・ユーザリクエストにより探索条件変更が行われた場合、探索機能は指定されたルート種類で探索すること。
＜JP/ROW＞
・ When adding, replacing, or deleting destinations and stops, if there is a transit point that has already passed, ignore the passing stopover and search. However, the transit point will remain displayed on the map.
・目的地、経由地の追加、入れ替え、削除を行った際、通過済みの経由地がある場合は通過済み経由地を無視して探索を行う。ただし、通過済み経由地は地図上に表示したままとする。
・ Do not fail to search unless there is a ferry route or the route does not exist.
・フェリー航路等がなく経路が存在しない場合以外は、探索失敗しないこと。
・ Up to 10 destinations and up to 5 transit points can be set.
・目的地は最大10件、通過点は最大5件まで設定可能とする。
＜NA/EU/AU＞
・ Up to 5 destinations can be set for Non-EV routing.
・ In case of EV routing, as many destinations/stops can be added as need to complete the route.
<ALL 全仕向>▲1.60
・ SDK shall contain an API that allows for the route segments between each stop/waypoint to appear in different colors. (Colors are configurable).
・ SDK shall contain an API that allows for the order of waypoints to be reordered. The reordered waypoint order can be sent from the server.
・ Navi shall add a waypoint before destination

children.heading_text: ・ User can see updated route which covers waypoint
children.content:
・ ETA to 1st waypoint is presented to user

・ Show modal pop-up with an error note ▲1.60 when user tries to add destination which is farther than 3200 miles or 5000km.▲1.60(distance configurable).
・ Show modal pop-up with an error note ▲1.60 when user tries to add waypoint that exceeds the max route length (distance configurable). The modal pop-up has two options, either to start a new route or to cancel. When customer selects “New Route, cancel the existing route and show screen to new destination. Customer shall select “Go Now” to start new trip. When customer selects “Cancel”, go back to previous active route.

children.heading_text: 5－6． Traffic jam consideration search service　渋滞考慮探索サービス
5－6－2． Basic operation (normal system)　基本動作（正常系）
Service Overview　サービス概要
children.content:
Traffic jam consideration search service by < traffic information use (DRGS)＞
・ Notify the user if traffic information changes during route guidance.
・ In the case of manual setting of the avoidance route, the difference information is linked to the avoidance route and displayed in the balloon.
Depending on the situation, the user can choose whether to switch to a new route by route or blowout touch.
・ If the user does not select a route for a certain period of time, guide the original route.
＜交通情報利用による渋滞考慮探索サービス(DRGS)＞
・経路案内中に交通情報が変化した場合、ユーザに通知する。
・回避ルート手動設定の場合、回避ルートに差分情報を紐付けて吹出で表示する。
・ユーザは状況に応じて、ルートまたは吹出タッチにより新しい経路に切替えるか選択することができる。
・ユーザが一定時間ルート選択を行わない場合は元ルートのまま案内する。

children.heading_text: Service requirements　サービス要件
children.content:
< Traffic jam consideration search service by traffic information use DRGS＞
＜交通情報利用による渋滞考慮探索サービス(DRGS)＞
・ The search function determines whether or not the latest route should be presented to the user in consideration of traffic information.
・ 探索機能は交通情報を考慮して最新の経路をユーザに提示すべきか否かを判断すること。
・ When presenting the latest route to the user, make sure that the user notices it, such as by producing audio.
・ 最新の経路をユーザーに提示する場合は、音声を出す等、ユーザに気付いてもらう工夫をすること。
・ In the case of manual setting of avoidance route, the original route and the avoidance route can be confirmed.
・ 回避ルート手動設定の場合は、元ルートと回避ルートが確認できるようにすること。
・ Display the shortened time of the avoidance route (difference time from the original route) with a blowout linked to the avoidance route.
・ 回避ルートの短縮時間(元ルートとの差分時間)を回避ルートに紐付けて吹出で表示すること。
・ SDK shall use real time traffic information data, road closures and traffic jam and traffic incidents.
・ SDK shall have the ability to detect when route should be updated due to changes in time restrictions and reroute accordingly.
・ Example: While driving on the highway, recommendation to get off highway and then get on highway needs to be avoided.
・ Display the balloon position so that it does not cover the current location, destination, or route.
・ 吹出位置は、現在地・目的地・ルートに被らないように表示すること。
・ Routes should be displayed in different route colors.
・ 2ルートは異なるルート色で表示すること。
・ Devise a display method so that the current route and the latest route can be understood by the user.
・ 現在の経路、最新の経路がユーザーに分かるように表示方法を工夫すること。
・ You can switch the selected route by touching (short pressing) the balloon / route.
・ 吹出/ルートをタッチ(短押し)することで選択ルートの切替ができること。
・ Once a new route is selected, it will prevent the rerouting for a certain period of time (preventing the route change time to be considered by each company from flickering and not confusing the user)
・ 一旦新しいルートが選択された場合、一定時間(ルート変更のばたつきを防ぎ、ユーザが混乱しない
程度の時間を各社で検討)は交通情報による渋滞考慮探索を行わないこと。
・ The range of consideration of traffic information shall be within 200 km square around the current location.
Tune regulatory information, traffic jam information, etc. to an appropriate range (range that affects the user).
・ For example, traffic jams 200 km away may be resolved by the time the user arrives, so it is outside the consideration range. * However, traffic information obtained from the Center must follow the center's designated range.
・ 交通情報の考慮範囲は、現在地周辺200km四方以内とする。
規制情報や渋滞情報などに対して、適切な範囲(ユーザに影響のある範囲)にチューニングすること。
例えば、200km先の渋滞はユーザが到着するまでに解消する可能性があるため考慮範囲外とするなど。※ただし、センターから取得した交通情報についてはセンター指定範囲に従うこと。
・ Search can be performed taking into account link travel time information by direction.
・ 方向別リンク旅行時間情報も考慮した探索を行えること。（日本のみ）
・ When considering regulatory information, consider the occurrence period, and do not consider regulatory information outside the period in route search.
Also, even if the period straddles the year, make sure that it is considered correctly from the end of the year to the beginning of the year.
・ 規制情報を考慮する際には発生期間を考慮し、期間外の規制情報はルート探索で考慮しないこと。
また期間が年を跨ぐ場合も年末から年始にかけて正しく考慮されるようにすること。
・ If a better route becomes available after original route was given due to changes in traffic information, it can be presented to the user as the latest route. For example, if the traffic jam on the main line is resolved, present a route through the main line instead of a side road.
・ 交通情報の変化によって元ルートよりも早いルートができた場合、最新のルートとしてユーザに提示できること。例えば、本線上の渋滞が解消した場合、側道ではなく本線を通るルートを提示すること。
・
・ Consider traffic information (traffic information for each right and left turn) according to the direction of travel, and search for routes.
・ 進行方向別の交通情報（右左折毎の交通情報）を考慮し、ルート探索を行うこと。
・ Consider Jam Lifetime and search for routes.
・ JamLifetimeを考慮し、ルート探索を行うこと。
・ Suggest a new route only if the changed route arrives at the destination earlier.
・ より確実に変更後のルートの方が早く目的地に到着する場合だけ、新ルートを提案すること。
・ The new route should be to read aloud how quickly you can get to your destination compared to the original route. If the change in fee is also involved, the change in fee shall also be notified.　▲1.20
・ 新ルートは、元ルートと比べて、どれだけ早く目的地に行けるかの音声読み上げをすること。その際、料金の変更も伴う場合は、料金の変化についても通知すること。
Traffic information that takes into account the transit time can be used.
・ 通過時間を考慮した交通情報を利用できること。
・ To be able to switch to a new route by voice.
・ 音声で、新ルートへの切り替えを可能とすること。
・ Popup for new route proposal is given when system detects increase of ETA by at least 5min (configurable) and faster route is found.
・ 5分以上ETAが早くなるルートが見つかった場合に、新しいルートをポップアップで提案すること。
・ User can switch to this route by tapping on the popup.
・ ▲1.60
・ユーザはポップアップに触れることでルートを切り替えることができる。▲1.60

Depending on the situation, the user can choose whether to switch to a new route by route or blowout touch.

Service Overview　サービス概要
children.content:

children.heading_text: 5－8． E-toll guidance service
5－8－2． Basic operation (normal system)　基本動作（正常系）
Service Overview　サービス概要
children.content:
South African highways have an "E-toll" system that charges for passing through gates like a Japanese ETC. Information on the "E-toll" gate is maintained in the base data, and the E-toll guidance service is provided on the road where the "E-toll" gate and the general tollgate can be distinguished.
There are two types of infrastructure: "E-toll" dedicated lanes and "E-toll" gates.
南アフリカの高速道路には、日本のETCのようなゲートを通過すると料金が課金されるシステム"e-toll"がある。"e-toll"ゲートの情報が基データに整備されており、"e-toll"ゲートと一般の料金所が識別できる道路において、e-toll案内サービスを提供する。
インフラには、"e-toll"専用レーンと"e-toll"ゲートの2つの種類がある。
" e-toll" dedicated lane
The toll booth has cash payment lanes and "E-toll" lanes.
The "E-toll" lane explicitly pays the fee because there is a bar when passing through the gate.
・"e-toll"専用レーン
料金所に現金支払いのレーンと"e-toll"専用のレーンがある。
"e-toll"専用レーンは、ゲートを通過する際にバーが存在するため利用者が料金を明示的に支払う。
"e-toll" gate
There is no cash payment, and you will be charged automatically after passing through the gate, and you will be invoiced at a later date.
* Vehicles without "E-tag" are judged by license plates when passing through the gate.
The "E-toll" gate may not be noticed and should be notified to the user.
・"e-toll"ゲート
現金支払いは無く、ゲートを通過したら自動的に課金され、後日請求書が送られてくる。
※"e-tag"非搭載車はゲート通過時にナンバープレートで判断される。
"e-toll"ゲートは気付かない可能性もあるため、ユーザーに通知する必要がある。
In the case of a route passing through the E-toll gate, it is possible to recognize whether it passes through the E-toll gate when searching for the route by informing it at the full route screen.
▲1.70

children.heading_text: 5－12． Custom HOV/HOT ▲1.10 Route Service
5－12－2． Basic operation (normal system)
Service Overview
children.content:
▲1.10
・ Propose a route that takes into account the number of passengers in the vehicle at any given time. ▲1.10

children.heading_text: Service requirements
children.content:
▲1.10
* User preferences/ feature data should be held for each user, not per vehicle.
・ Vehicle shall be able to detect the number of passengers and inform routing system when number of passengers changes.
・ HOV/HOT lanes shall be allowed when number of required passengers is met.
・ HOV/HOT lanes shall be avoided when number of required passengers is not met.
・ If route is currently using HOV/HOT lane and the required number of passengers decreases, the route must be recalculated to avoid the HOV/HOT lanes.
Note: The number of passengers will be based on the number of seat belts fastened in the vehicle as reported on CAN bus. The minimum number of passengers required for a given HOV/HOT lane should come from map DB
Seatbelt Signal (MET1S47) is periodically detected and considered failed if the Navi fails to detect the signal 10 times consecutively. In the case of communication failure, number of passengers in the vehicle is assumed to be 1. Once CAN communication is restored and Navi successfully detects the signal, number of passengers shall be detected based on seatbelt signal. ▲1.10

children.heading_text: 5－14． Predictive/Preferred Route Learning Service ▲1.10
5－14－2． Basic operation (normal system)
Service Overview
children.content:
A route learning service that records both route shape preferences (▲3.10)  in order to predict where a user will drive and provide them with routes tailored to their personal preferences.
- The user sets the destination and makes the initial search.
・ (▲3.10) Preferred routes are considered in the center route.
・ SDK shall contain an API between Navi Server and Toyota Server to allow access to this data.
・ When managing the route learning result on the center side, there are no cases where data is uploaded from multiple vehicles of the same user. All 24MM navi subscriptions are VIN based, so all route learning data per user can only be uploaded from a single VIN. Route learning results can be managed on center side for multiple users from a single VIN.
・ In response to requests from users (opt-out requests, initialization of personal information, etc.), they are accumulated in the local / center.
The ability to discard data from route learning results is possible

children.heading_text: Service requirements
children.content:
・ The search function is to memorize the running road from the departure point to the final destination.
・ The search function is a breakaway link when the user runs away from the initial path,
・ Record the information in between the user when returning to the original route after leaving the route.
・ Routes begin to be learned as "Predictive" when the following criteria is met:
o
o
o (▲3.10)
・ Routes begin to be learned as "Preferred" when the following criteria is met:
o Route is driven from same Departure Point to Destination 3x (parameter is configurable) – or comparable logic.
o
o (▲3.10)
Once a route has been learned as a preferred route, the above criteria must be met again for that same preferred route to be re-learned into a new preferred route
・ If there are multiple learning routes for the same departure point or destination, consider the departure point or destination with a unique day and time - or comparable logic.
・ When updating the map, determine the link shape and update the learning result.
・ When reflecting the learning route, consider the route preference (search conditions for user selection) and reflect it.
